review_gates.notes: Splitting the automation profile into a standalone repo; generic-agent adapter is the portable surface.

automation kernel, profile-index entry, and its 6 member skills are copied as authoritative sources. Skills shared with core-ops (project-ops-router, sidecar-task-runner) are also copied; the depends_on relationship is documented in profile-index.
Direct-entrypoint selection semantics are carried over; the profile-first / full-root delegation contract belongs to core-ops, not automation.
Source inventory, file copy list, and exclusion list reviewed before scaffolding the destination repo. depends_on: core-ops noted in destination profile-index.
Pre-split: acceptance_checks in source repo. Post-split: re-run exporter, validator, inventory script, and confirm automation references no ML paper-lifecycle skill in the destination repo.

Privacy audit at schemas/skill-kernel/repo-split/automation-privacy-audit-2026-05-28.json reports status: passed with zero hits over the proposed split set, including credential / token / SSH transcript patterns.
Inventory + audit confirm no credential, auth token, scheduler API token, or concrete remote cluster path appears in any file the scaffolder would copy.

privacy_audit.notes: Programmatic audit at schemas/skill-kernel/repo-split/automation-privacy-audit-2026-05-28.json scans every included file for credential / personal-identifier / concrete sidecar / SSH-key patterns and reports 0 hits over 63 checked paths.

rollback.restore_strategy: Record destination repo URL, commit hash before scaffolding, and exact file copy list; on rollback, delete the destination repo (or revert its first commit) and restore the source profile-index entry from the rollback record.
